speech.text: Welcome! Work hard, pay what you owe, and walk free. The ledger never lies.
Fees are a normal part of any arrangement. It's all in the ledger.
Your family too? Wonderful. Many hands make light debt.
Accidents are unfortunate. Medical care isn't free, you understand.
Costs are up everywhere. Nothing I can do. It's all in the ledger.
Isn't it marvelous? My husband gives these people purpose.
Why do they look so tired? The desert air is wonderfully refreshing.
Your wife should be more careful. Kiln repairs are terribly expensive.
Do tell your boy to mind the equipment. Replacement molds take weeks to arrive.
Shovel the mud, mold it, bake it, ship it. Stand still at your station and your hands learn the rhythm.
The bricks pile up. The truck takes them, the ledger counts them.
The kiln takes more than it gives. Watch your family.
Every brick on that truck pays down your debt. I've been counting mine for thirty years.
The price drops when the owner says it drops. No one has ever seen this 'market'.
You came alone? They all come alone, at first.
Family at the same kiln can share the work. Stay close to them.
We couldn't wait at home any longer. Let me help - we'll pay it off together.
The burn will heal. I can still work. I have to.
Father! I'm strong enough to work now. Show me what to do.
I can still carry bricks with one hand. Don't look at me like that.
